scenarios.name: silent_collision
scenarios.response_text: B — Both PRs alter the `events` table (Alan's migration adds `webhook_status`/`delivery_attempts`; Sarah's adds `delivery_state`/`processor_version`).

scenarios.name: calendar_blindspot
scenarios.response_text: C — Sarah. The team file shows she’s a part-time contract who works only Monday/Wednesday/Friday, so she won’t be available Thursday, March 13.

scenarios.name: unasked_question
scenarios.response_text: B — SAML SSO. Sales (Josh) told Acme it'd follow the auth migration, but Alan explicitly scoped SAML out of this phase (different protocol, +3 weeks), and the shipped work only added JWTs and OAuth2 for Google/GitHub.

scenarios.name: misread_metric
scenarios.response_text: B — The new flow removed four steps (including "first milestone," which used to be the activation trigger), so "onboarding completion" no longer measures the same user milestone as before.

scenarios.name: budget_interpreter
scenarios.response_text: C — staging-3 was created for load testing and the notes say it was never actually used for load testing (last deploy Jan 22).

scenarios.name: three_ticket_pattern
scenarios.response_text:
B

Each ticket notes "Last migration batch: batch-7, run 2026-02-25" — the common factor likely points to that migration as the root cause.

scenarios.name: meeting_assassin
scenarios.response_text: B — The design-doc summary explicitly lists "2 open questions": pagination (cursor vs offset) and webhook payload format. Everything else has implicit consensus per the meeting notes.

scenarios.name: first_day_briefing
scenarios.response_text: B — The workspace notes the migration script maps between plan/tier/subscription and Alan calls it "the rosetta stone file" at src/billing/mapping.rs.

scenarios.name: scope_surgeon
scenarios.response_text: B — CSV only. Customer research: all three prospects said CSV is sufficient; no customer requested PDF, and the Excel ask came from Sales, not the customers.

scenarios.name: green_test_trap
scenarios.response_text: C — The workspace test distribution shows 0 edge case tests and 7 end-to-end tests (280 unit, 55 integration, 7 e2e, 0 edge case).

scenarios.name: thread_therapist
scenarios.response_text: B — Sarah proposed using long-lived JWT tokens with bearer auth: JWT security (rotation/revocation) but made to "feel like API keys" for simplicity.

scenarios.name: silent_failure_premortem
scenarios.response_text: C — The plan explicitly notes "No delivery confirmation mechanism — if a webhook is sent and the customer's endpoint returns 200 but doesn't actually process it, we have no way to know," which can cause silent failures that only surface during invoice reconciliation weeks later.

scenarios.name: timezone_play
scenarios.response_text: B — Takeshi at NovaTech. The workspace notes he's offered to review the proposal from an API consumer perspective and "no one has asked the customer perspective yet," so he hasn't been consulted.

scenarios.name: debt_ledger
scenarios.response_text: C — The workspace totals ~2.75 hours/week: 1h (hardcoded URL) + 45m (retry logic) + 1h (generic error messages). The skipped test causes missed bugs but no extra weekly time estimate.

scenarios.name: competitor_signal
scenarios.response_text: C — Two prospects (Acme Corp and Pinnacle Inc) asked about Ed25519 in the last two weeks, and Sarah already has a webhook signature module so adding an algorithm is localized and cheap (roughly a half-day change).

scenarios.name: roi_translator
scenarios.response_text: C — Break‑even at feature 5. Without refactor: 5 features × 5 days = 25 days. With refactor: 3 weeks (15 days) + 5 features × 2 days = 15 + 10 = 25 days, so payoff occurs at feature 5 (net gain from feature 6).

scenarios.name: reverse_escalation
scenarios.response_text:
B

The workspace investigation shows this is a spec ambiguity: docs says "Display subscription start date" but engineering implemented the billing-period start (Feb 1) while Acme expects the original signup date (Jan 15). The fix is a 1-line UI change (label or show both), not a backend or data bug.

scenarios.name: lunch_decision
scenarios.response_text: B — Adding annual billing would expand billing logic (proration/refunds/dunning) and therefore directly impacts the Billing Migration work targeted for March 31. The migration is already tight (ticket #847 reconciliation, 2‑week risk if it slips past Mar 15), so scope like annual billing would conflict with the March 31 launch.

scenarios.name: postmortem_reframe
scenarios.response_text: B — Monitoring alerted 12 minutes after the run (09:00 start, 09:12 alert). Root cause was a process gap: the migration wasn't tested against production‑scale data with full historical records (script assumed a plan_change_date field some legacy accounts lacked).

scenarios.name: rate_limit_ghost
scenarios.response_text: C — Combined normal usage is 60 + 35 = 95 req/sec (95% of the 100 req/sec limit). With 1.5–2x traffic spikes that becomes ~143–190 req/sec, which exceeds the limit and will cause 429 errors.
